summary: Carry the authored representation forward on custom-unit re-import — `import_custom_unit` clobbers hand-authored requirement/satisfies tables with a provenance-only header

per.section: Drift / apply — three states, never two (the hard core)

edit.description: `import_custom_unit` (import.rs:362) writes a provenance-ONLY header (add_provenance over a fresh DocumentMut, import.rs:391-398), NEVER merging an existing surface layer — unlike `import_frontmatter_member` which calls `existing_surface_member`/`Member::carry_representation` (import.rs:240-242) to merge-not-clobber. So a re-import or drifted-body `re-add` of a changed custom-kind source (a spec) rebuilds the document from source and SILENTLY WIPES the hand-authored `[requirement.*]`/`[satisfies.*]` tables (the 17-join intent↔architecture trace, 7d94abc). Fix: before emitting, read any existing surface member's header at `out_dir/<BODY>.md` and carry every authored clause block forward (everything but `[provenance]`, which is freshly stamped), so the custom-unit path merges-not-clobbers exactly as the frontmatter path does (`specs/architecture/20-surface.md`, 'three states, never two' — merge rather than clobber). Custom units build a raw `Document` (no `Member`), so lift the non-provenance header tables from the parsed existing document and re-emit them above the new provenance; byte-faithful body unchanged.

tests.asserts: re-add (and re-import) of a custom-unit source whose body drifted preserves the hand-authored `[requirement.*]`/`[satisfies.*]` header tables — they are carried forward, not clobbered by the provenance-only header — while `[provenance]` is re-stamped to the new import hash; re-add of an unchanged unit is idempotent.

acceptance: A custom-kind (spec) member carrying authored `[requirement.*]`/`[satisfies.*]` survives a re-import/re-add of its drifted source with the tables intact (only `[provenance]` re-stamped); the frontmatter and custom-unit import paths are now symmetric on carry-forward; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Drained from inbox (2026-07-02): the 17-join intent↔architecture trace (7d94abc) rests on `import_custom_unit` NOT wiping the authored tables, but it does — the sole import/re-add path for every custom kind writes a bare provenance header, no carry_representation. Latent (check is clean today, tables are on disk); fires the next re-import. Until it ships, 05-model's source owning-spec column stays the reconcile-durable copy (its DRY dedup is contingent on this slice).

summary: Read verbs `why`/`requirements` must range over the composed requirement namespace `check` gates — today they read the assembly roster only, misreporting member-published joins as dangling

per.section: Decision: the CLI gains a read family — `why` and `requirements`

edit.description: `roster(layer)` (read.rs:117-121) returns `layer.requirements()` — the ASSEMBLY roster only, blind to member-published `[requirement.*]`. The read family is defined as traversals 'over data `check` already computes' (`specs/architecture/20-surface.md`), and `check` composes the ONE namespace = assembly ∪ member-published (main.rs:648 `union_published_requirements`, `specs/architecture/10-contracts.md` 'a requirement's publisher is any authored surface document'). Consequence: `requirements` lists 2 of 19, and `why <member>` narrates every trace `satisfies` as 'This link dangles' while `check` is green — a falsehood about a healthy graph, worse than a gap. Fix: `why`/`requirements` walk the COMPOSED roster (assembly ∪ member-published), not `roster(layer)`. Accept the composed `&BTreeMap<String, Requirement>` from the caller (main builds it via the same union) rather than re-deriving; the read `Member` view drops `published_requirements`, so the composition source stays main's.

The `Why` (main.rs:425) and `Requirements` (main.rs:477) command arms must build the composed requirement namespace the gate builds and pass it into `read::why`/`read::requirements`, replacing read's internal assembly-only `roster(layer)`. Reuse `union_published_requirements` (main.rs:923) over the same feature stream the gate assembles (skill ⊕ rule ⊕ each custom kind's members) so read and gate agree by construction — the `Requirements` arm today loads only `custom_members`, so it must also gather the built-in + custom features the union reads. Emit the composed roster (collisions are the gate's finding, not re-reported by a read).

tests.asserts: `temper requirements` lists member-published requirements (a fixture publishing `[requirement.*]` on a surface member appears in the roster overview and in a named `requirements <name>` detail with its satisfier set); `temper why <member>` narrates a `satisfies` link to a member-published requirement as FILLED with its rationale, never 'This link dangles', matching a green `check`.

acceptance: `temper why` and `temper requirements` range over the composed assembly ∪ member-published roster `check` gates: `requirements` lists every published obligation (19, not 2) and `why 45-governance` reports its trace joins as live, not dangling; the verbs stay projections (zero exit, no gate); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Drained from inbox (2026-07-02). The misreport is worse than a gap — the verb states a falsehood about a green graph (check composes assembly ∪ member-published at main.rs:648; the read verbs at read.rs:117 do not). Disjoint from CUSTOM-UNIT-REPRESENTATION-CARRY (import.rs) — this touches read.rs/main.rs/read_verbs.rs, so both stay parallel-safe as `open`.

summary: Add the `memory` built-in kind — CLAUDE.md family (no frontmatter, dual root locus, `@path` imports); the first pure-data managed kind, unlocking the CLAUDE.md-onto-surface flip

per.section: Two categories of kind — ownership, not mechanism

gate.reason: the curated kinds/claude-code/memory/KIND.md + packages/memory.anthropic/PACKAGE.md are outside build's fence (curated std-lib — a human authors them, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/memory); and the no-frontmatter projection format + the `@path` edge extraction primitive are deliberate closed-vocab additions a human must sanction before the engine wiring can land

new.description: The embedded built-in `memory` KIND.md (curated — humans author it, outside build's fence), under its provider beside kinds/claude-code/skill and kinds/claude-code/rule with a `provider = "claude-code"` line. Format facts per specs/architecture/15-kinds.md: no frontmatter, `@`-imports, dual root locus (project `./CLAUDE.md` + user `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`). Declares a no-frontmatter projection format, markdown-structure extraction over the body, and `@path` imports as declared edges. build.rs auto-embeds it.

The memory kind's built-in package `memory.anthropic` (curated product territory, outside build's fence), embedded with the std-lib by build.rs. Decidable clauses only over the memory format, per-clause `source` citations to the Claude Code memory docs (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory); the `@path` imports resolve as a referential clause over the declared edge (never a body grep — law 8).

Fixture-driven proof mirroring tests/rule_contract.rs: broken/clean memory fixtures through the generic adapter + loader, plus an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence over a no-frontmatter unit.

edit.description: Engine wiring (inside the fence): add "memory" to BUILTIN_KINDS (kind.rs:30); add the no-frontmatter projection-format vocab entry beside `yaml-frontmatter` (the declared-adapter face); add an `@path`-import edge extraction primitive to the closed `Primitive` vocab (enum kind.rs:545 + parse_primitive load-guard kind.rs:1171). All are deliberate closed-vocab additions harvested because the memory kind demands them (specs/architecture/15-kinds.md Decision: extraction is a closed algebra).

Add a `MEMORY_PACKAGE = "memory.anthropic"` const and register the memory kind→package binding beside SKILL_PACKAGE/RULE_PACKAGE (builtin.rs:37-44). build.rs auto-embeds the PACKAGE.md; no manual embed edit.

The extractor helpers behind the memory format: an `@path`-import reader over the markdown body yielding declared edges (mirroring body_headings/body_sections), and the no-frontmatter unit read face (empty frontmatter map, body is the whole file).

The generic adapter's no-frontmatter format path: split/emit a memory member whose on-disk artifact carries no YAML frontmatter (the `yaml-frontmatter` adapter assumes a fence), byte-deterministic re-emission of the body.

tests.asserts: the memory package's decidable clauses fire on broken fixtures and stay silent on clean; a no-frontmatter memory member imports and re-emits idempotently (insta snapshot); an `@path` import resolves as a declared edge and a dangling one is a coverage finding.

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches the CLAUDE.md family to `memory.anthropic`; `temper import` projects memory files as no-frontmatter members idempotently via the generic adapter; the `@path` edge primitive builds the memory graph; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green. (Parked: not built until the curated KIND.md/PACKAGE.md are human-authored and the format/primitive additions sanctioned.)
notes: Drained from the open-questions CLAUDE.md-bootstrap recipe (DFA shipped, so it's fileable). Memory is the flip-ceremony proof — shipping it moves CLAUDE.md onto the surface. Curated KIND.md/PACKAGE.md are outside build's fence: a human authors them (cited code.claude.com/docs/en/memory), then engine wiring lands. NB `@path` is its own edge primitive, NOT EXTRACTION-VOCAB-GAPS's fenced/key-path — that entry stays deferred, not revived here.

summary: Extend the extraction algebra with the two primitives 15-kinds.md lists but code lacks — a `fenced` block extractor and key-path (nested) `field` lookup — held until a consumer kind needs them

per.section: The extraction algebra — the soundness boundary, as data

gate.reason: no consumer — the extraction vocabulary is harvested from the built-in kinds and extended deliberately (15-kinds.md Decision: extraction is a closed algebra); no current kind (skill/rule) reads a fenced block or a nested key-path, so adding either now is speculative scope with no kind to exercise it. Revive when a kind demands it — a JSON-manifest kind's nested key-path field, or a spec/class kind's fenced-block extraction. (NB memory's `@path` is a separate edge primitive, not one of these — MEMORY-KIND does not revive this.)

edit.description: Two closed-vocabulary additions to `Primitive` (`specs/architecture/15-kinds.md`): (1) a `Fenced` primitive extracting fenced code-block content — the markdown-structure extractor the spec lists (`a fenced block`) but the enum omits (today: field/headings/sections/line_count/placement, kind.rs:545-565); (2) make `Primitive::Field` honor the key-path its own doc-comment promises — the impl currently does a flat `unit.frontmatter.get(key)` (kind.rs:588) so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read. Both extend the closed-vocab load-guard's accepted primitive set (parse_primitive, kind.rs:1171). This entry owns both edits so they don't collide in parallel worktrees.

The extractor helpers behind the two primitives: a `body_fenced_blocks`-style reader over the markdown body (mirroring `body_headings`/`body_sections`), and key-path traversal for the field extractor (walk nested TOML/JSON tables to the leaf before `json_to_feature`, kind-preserving per the `type` lattice, `specs/architecture/10-contracts.md`).

tests.asserts: a `fenced` primitive yields the fenced block's content and skips prose; a key-path `field` reads a nested value and is absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve; both preserve the source scalar kind.

acceptance: `Primitive::Fenced` and a nested-key-path `Primitive::Field` are in the closed vocabulary, load-guarded, and extract over a `Unit`; a KIND.md declaring either reads the intended feature; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green. (Deferred: not built until a kind consumes one.)
notes: Disk re-confirmed both gaps: `Primitive` = field/headings/sections/line_count/placement (kind.rs:545-565); `Field` reads flat `frontmatter.get(key)` (kind.rs:588), no key-path; load-guard parse_primitive at kind.rs:1171. Neither breaks anything today (no kind uses them). ONE entry — both touch src/kind.rs + src/extract.rs. MEMORY-KIND does NOT revive this: `@path` is a distinct edge primitive, not fenced/key-path.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` as a member document, read through the generic loader and checked by the embedded `agent.anthropic` package

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; and post-adapter-wave the agent kind is mostly curated data (a KIND.md + PACKAGE.md, both outside build's fence) with near-zero engine code — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The embedded built-in `agent` KIND.md (curated — humans author it; outside build's fence), under its provider beside kinds/claude-code/skill and kinds/claude-code/rule with a `provider = "claude-code"` line: `governs = { root = '.claude/agents', glob = '*.md' }`, the declared `yaml-frontmatter` projection format + unit shape, composed extraction over the `name`/`description`/optional `tools`/`model` fields plus markdown structure. build.rs auto-embeds it — no engine extractor.

The agent kind's built-in package `agent.anthropic` (curated product territory, outside build's fence), embedded with the std-lib by build.rs. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`. Per-clause `source` citations to the Claude Code subagent docs.

Fixture-driven proof mirroring tests/rule_contract.rs: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence — all through the generic adapter + loader, no per-kind engine code.

edit.description: Add `"agent"` to the `BUILTIN_KINDS` name-list (kind.rs:30) so a `[kind.agent]` binding reads as a built-in layer, not a custom kind.

Add the `AGENT_PACKAGE = "agent.anthropic"` const beside SKILL_PACKAGE/RULE_PACKAGE (builtin.rs:37-44) and register its kind→package binding. build.rs auto-embeds the PACKAGE.md; no manual embed edit.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the `agent.anthropic` package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently via the generic frontmatter adapter; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Declared-adapter architecture: no src/agent.rs IR — the generic adapter projects it. Curated kinds/claude-code/agent/KIND.md + packages/agent.anthropic/PACKAGE.md sit outside build's fence; engine wiring is near-zero (add "agent" to BUILTIN_KINDS kind.rs:30 + an AGENT_PACKAGE const/binding in builtin.rs; build.rs auto-embeds). Adapter wave drained (DFA shipped). Stays deferred: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under requirements/satisfies.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/architecture/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.

summary: Add CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md — the two missing launch community docs (two-sided AI-authorship disclosure; vuln-reporting evidence bar)

per.section: The community surface — small, honest, gated

gate.reason: build's writable fence (.flume/chain.ts BUILD_WRITABLE_PATHS) lists only README/CHANGELOG/AGENTS/LICENSE among root docs — no root CONTRIBUTING.md/SECURITY.md glob — so build would bail; a human must widen the fence (chain.ts is human territory) and enable private vulnerability reporting (a repo setting) before build can author these

new.description: Small and honest per the Decision: how to file a good issue, 'search first', PRs by prior discussion, and the two-sided AI-authorship policy (inbound AI-assisted contributions welcome WITH disclosure; the contributor must understand and defend the change without the assistant). Expectation-setting, not an invitation to co-maintain.

Vulnerability-reporting policy with an explicit evidence bar — a report must demonstrate, not speculate (the 'unverified AI-generated vuln reports are the new spam' lesson). Points at private vulnerability reporting (the GitHub repo setting a human enables).

acceptance: CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md exist at root, matching specs/intent/55-offering.md's launch-set Decisions (two-sided disclosure; demonstrate-not-speculate); the fence has been widened so build's entry-fence preflight admits them.
notes: Launch set (specs/intent/55-offering.md). Already on disk: README, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE-MIT/APACHE, issue forms (.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug,feature,config}.yml). Missing only CONTRIBUTING.md + SECURITY.md. Both root-level per the spec but outside build's root-docs fence — parked on a human fence-widen (chain.ts) + enabling private reporting. Parked entries are skipped by the entry-fence gate, so this files cleanly.
